feature_title: Plan - Third Audio Engine Option Evaluation

plan.title: Plan - Third Audio Engine Option Evaluation
plan.objective: Evaluate whether a native HTML5 direct audio-engine mode should be accepted as a viable third option, with explicit decision gates covering product fit, technical risk, and operational sustainability before any implementation commitment.
in_scope: Define candidate scope for native HTML5 direct playback, required baseline behaviors, explicit exclusions, and evaluation gates needed to determine if deeper implementation planning is justified.
out_of_scope: This plan does not authorize code changes, rollout sequencing, migration execution, or production enablement. Existing engine behavior and roadmap commitments remain unchanged until a separate implementation proposal is approved.
references: Playback reliability SLOs and user-impact metrics

acceptance_criteria: All baseline user-facing playback requirements are mapped to supported, partial, or unsupported status
Top risk areas are explicitly identified with confidence level annotations
Each go/no-go criterion has an objective pass/fail threshold
Evidence collection approach is reviewed for repeatability and bias reduction
references: Playback reliability SLOs and user-impact metrics

acceptance_criteria: Recommendation is traceable to documented gate outcomes
Open risks and follow-up questions are captured with ownership suggestions

tasks.summary: Run verification for resumed soundspan work
acceptance_criteria: Run the repo-defined ACM verification tests before completion.

source_plan.feature_title: Plan - Third Audio Engine Option Evaluation

pre_spec_outline.purpose_goals: Evaluate whether a native HTML5 direct audio-engine mode should be accepted as a viable third option, with explicit decision gates covering product fit, technical risk, and operational sustainability before any implementation commitment.
pre_spec_outline.non_goals: This plan does not authorize code changes, rollout sequencing, migration execution, or production enablement. Existing engine behavior and roadmap commitments remain unchanged until a separate implementation proposal is approved.

spec_outline.summary: Assess a potential third audio-engine mode (native HTML5 direct) against current engine options with clear go/no-go criteria and evidence requirements.

full_spec_outline.objective: Define candidate scope for native HTML5 direct playback, required baseline behaviors, explicit exclusions, and evaluation gates needed to determine if deeper implementation planning is justified.
full_spec_outline.deliverable: Define candidate scope for native HTML5 direct playback, required baseline behaviors, explicit exclusions, and evaluation gates needed to determine if deeper implementation planning is justified.
acceptance_criteria: Complete deliverable: Define candidate scope for native HTML5 direct playback, required baseline behaviors, explicit exclusions, and evaluation gates needed to determine if deeper implementation planning is justified.

refined_spec.summary: Convert the evaluation into measurable gates that determine whether the native HTML5 direct option should be rejected, parked, or advanced to a later implementation proposal.

full_refined_spec.decision: Set threshold criteria for compatibility coverage, startup/seek reliability, buffering and recovery behavior, telemetry observability, and maintainability cost relative to existing engines, with documented pass/fail outcomes.
full_refined_spec.rationale: Set threshold criteria for compatibility coverage, startup/seek reliability, buffering and recovery behavior, telemetry observability, and maintainability cost relative to existing engines, with documented pass/fail outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: Validate decision: Set threshold criteria for compatibility coverage, startup/seek reliability, buffering and recovery behavior, telemetry observability, and maintainability cost relative to existing engines, with documented pass/fail outcomes.

implementation_plan.summary: Execute an evaluation-only process for the native HTML5 direct option and produce a decision package without committing to build work.

acceptance_criteria: All baseline user-facing playback requirements are mapped to supported, partial, or unsupported status
Top risk areas are explicitly identified with confidence level annotations
Each go/no-go criterion has an objective pass/fail threshold
Evidence collection approach is reviewed for repeatability and bias reduction
references: Playback reliability SLOs and user-impact metrics

acceptance_criteria: Recommendation is traceable to documented gate outcomes
Open risks and follow-up questions are captured with ownership suggestions
